metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug; driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Vibe keeps rich token + cost accounting in the per-session `meta.json` sidecar, rewritten after every turn. `stats` carries `session_prompt_tokens`, `session_completion_tokens`, `session_total_llm_tokens`, `last_turn_prompt_tokens`, `last_turn_completion_tokens`, `context_tokens`, `session_cost`, `input_price_per_million`, and `output_price_per_million` (confirmed live: session_total_llm_tokens=11000869, last_turn_prompt_tokens=135852, last_turn_completion_tokens=810, session_cost=16.7378535, input/output price 1.5/7.5). The agent itself performs the per-turn and cumulative accounting the scenario is about.

### daemon_capability: bug

The scenario's acceptance requires (1) TotalTokens > 0 after turn 1, (2) a MONOTONIC TotalTokens accumulation across turns (a per-turn contribution stream folded into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens), and (3) a cache-bucket distinction (CacheReadTokens > 0 with fresh input = 0 for a fully-cached turn).

The vibe adapter surfaces NONE of that accumulation. `parser.go`'s `applySidecar` emits, on `turn_done` only, `ev.Tokens = &TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens}` — a single context-utilization snapshot. `sidecar.go` reads ONLY `stats.context_tokens` (it ignores every other stats field). So:
- `context_tokens` is the CURRENT context-window size (== live `last_turn_total_tokens`), not the monotonic SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulator. It can DECREASE on a compaction, so it does not back the non-decreasing invariant the scenario asserts.
- No `PerTurnContribution` / `UsageBreakdown` (input/output) is ever emitted, so per-turn accumulation across requestId-equivalent boundaries is not reconstructed.
- No cost is emitted (parser never reads `session_cost` / prices; mistral-vibe is also absent from `core/pkg/capacity`), so `cost_nonzero` is false.
- No cache bucket is emitted (and Vibe's `stats` exposes no cache_read field to begin with — see caveat).

This is a `bug`, not `incapable`: the data EXISTS in the watched Source (`meta.json.stats`). A feasible live-tailing fix reads `last_turn_prompt_tokens` / `last_turn_completion_tokens` into a `PerTurnContribution{Usage:{Input,Output}}` on each `turn_done` and computes per-turn cost from `input_price_per_million` / `output_price_per_million`, folding into SessionMetrics.TotalTokens. Known limitation (documented in `sidecar.go`): the sidecar retains only the LAST turn's numbers, so per-turn cost is NOT reconstructable on BACKFILL of a finished transcript — only live-tailing sees each turn's `last_turn_*` before the next turn overwrites it. This is a known cost/accumulation follow-up.

Because the daemon is a bug on the spec-required accounting, the spec is marked `known_failing` while still asserting the correct three-turn lifecycle arc; the observation vector records current reality (`tokens_nonzero=true` from the context snapshot, `cost_nonzero=false`).

### driver_capability: gap:wait_turn

The recipe is a multi-turn interactive REPL sequence (send → wait_turn → sleep, three times) — the process must outlive the observation window across all three turns. The freshly scaffolded vibe interactive driver declares `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and STUBS `wait_turn`, so it cannot pace turn boundaries yet. Consistent with the sibling vibe interactive cells (basic-turn, task-list) which are all `gap:wait_turn`. `record` ports the wait_turn seam from a reference driver before driving.

## Route

daemon=bug + driver=gap:* → driver-gap (record ports the missing step, records known_failing, and the record subagent returns an issue payload for the cost/accumulation follow-up).
caveats: Cache-bucket sub-assertion (CacheReadTokens > 0; fresh input = 0 for a fully-cached turn) is unobservable regardless of a parser fix: Vibe's meta.json stats expose no cache_read/cache-hit field — only prompt/completion/context/total token counts. So even a fixed accumulator cannot surface the cache-bucket distinction; that part of the scenario is agent-side unavailable for vibe.

context_tokens (the one token field the parser DOES read) is a context-window snapshot, not a monotonic accumulator — it can shrink on compaction. tokens_nonzero=true is satisfied by it, but it does not itself prove the non-decreasing TotalTokens invariant.

PROVISIONAL on emission: whether a proc-<PID> presession row precedes the session_<...> birth is not confirmable from source; record settles it (single-birth adapter, modeled birth-at-start unpinned).
daemon_version recorded without the .dirty suffix (replaydata write leaves the tree dirty at authoring time).

sources.note: applySidecar emits ONLY ev.Tokens=&TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens} on turn_done — no PerTurnContribution, no UsageBreakdown (input/output/cache), no cost.

sidecarState + readSidecar read ONLY stats.context_tokens; every other stats field (session_total_llm_tokens, last_turn_prompt/completion_tokens, session_cost, prices) is ignored. Comment: on backfill only the final turn's numbers survive.

Live stats prove the data EXISTS: session_prompt_tokens, session_completion_tokens, session_total_llm_tokens=11000869, last_turn_prompt_tokens=135852, last_turn_completion_tokens=810, context_tokens=136662, session_cost=16.7378535, input/output_price_per_million=1.5/7.5. config.active_model=mistral-medium-3.5.
Freshly scaffolded: DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; wait_turn stubbed → driver=gap:wait_turn for the multi-turn recipe.
Template for the 3-turn token-accumulation recipe/spec (send/wait_turn/sleep x3) and the daemon=full reference the vibe bug is measured against.

preconditions: mistral-vibe (vibe) v2.19.0 on PATH and authenticated (Mistral API auth persisted on this machine)
irrlichd is recording — run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a daemon started with --record
no other vibe session shares the same cwd (PID is bound by cwd)
driver's wait_turn seam ported from a reference driver (currently stubbed — driver=gap:wait_turn)
setup: Create an empty cwd under <staging>/cwd/ (the driver launches vibe there); the session_<...> transcript dir is minted under ~/.vibe/logs/session/
No special model or context flags — default model (mistral-medium-3.5) is fine; the assertion is about token accumulation across turns, not which model produced the tokens

script.text: Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok
Reply with exactly the word: ok

verify: events.jsonl mints the session_<...> session in ready shortly after launch (single-birth; id from the transcript directory)
events.jsonl contains a pid_discovered event binding the vibe PID to the session (matched by cwd)
events.jsonl shows three ready→working→ready cycles on the same session (one per turn)
each turn_done carries Tokens.Total > 0 (from sidecar context_tokens)

KNOWN FAILING (daemon=bug): events.jsonl carries NO PerTurnContribution/UsageBreakdown and NO cost — the monotonic SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulation and cost the scenario requires are absent; the correct behavior would fold last_turn_prompt/completion_tokens + prices into a per-turn contribution
